summary: Session 5 of 5 (Docs, UAT, and release) - all orchestrator-side work is complete and cross-provider VERIFIED; the session is held open pending the two operator-only items: the UAT walk (6-item checklist authored this session) and the vsix-v0.35.0 tag-push authorization. What landed: (1) Docs pass - docs/concepts/tier-model.md now documents the guided Getting Started flow as the primary copilot-cli activation path with manual router-config.yaml editing as the fallback, and its evidence basis carries the M3 honesty note (Sets 078+079 validated on the SAME single personal seat; multi-seat/enterprise availability NOT validated; enterprise one-provider-family seats fail the >=2 check even when the flow works; POSIX kill unit-pinned only; config write process-crash-atomic, not power-loss-durable). Both READMEs describe the Full-tier 'Provider access' sub-choice + missing-CLI warning and carry the same full honesty caveat; the bundled getting-started template no longer claims DABBLER_* keys are required for Full (keys are the direct-API option; the Copilot CLI seat is the keyless alternative); cold-start goldens regenerated; the decomposition prompt was checked and needs no change (references tier only at spec-authoring level). (2) The 6-walk UAT checklist authored to the Set 078 bar (literal strings re-grounded from source, fresh Layer-2 counts quoted per walk, real-seat walks backed by the S2/S3 dogfoods, honest scope limits in Notes). (3) End-of-set multi-provider path-aware critique (gemini-2.5-pro VERIFIED clean + gpt-5.4 one Major contract-drift finding adjudicated accept-finding: its blocking premises were refuted by grep evidence, its real residue - a fictional 'Dabbler: Set Up Copilot Seat' command name in a test fixture - was fixed, suite 82/82; artifact saved raw, round lineage recorded). (4) Release scope confirmed extension-only (git diff a05c014..HEAD over ai_router/ + pyproject.toml is empty; router stays 0.28.0); extension bumped to 0.35.0 with CHANGELOG entry and repository-reference release-status/version-walk rows (publish honestly marked PENDING). (5) Suites: pytest 2483/5 skipped, tsc clean, Layer 2 1238 passing. Session verification (gpt-5-4, OpenAI, cross-provider): R1 two findings with no verdict token/severities, treated blocking per anti-laundering (S5-V-001 incomplete README honesty caveat; S5-V-002 stale generic key-requirement phrasing), both fixed; R2 narrow re-verify VERIFIED, both ledger ids RESOLVED.
verification_notes: Routed gate: REQUIRED (blast-radius, multi-module, breadth >=14 files, build-ci-config via package.json). Session verification on gpt-5-4 (OpenAI, cross-provider from the Claude orchestrator): R1 returned two findings with no verdict token and no severities - treated as blocking per the L-071-1 anti-laundering rule; ledger ids S5-V-001 and S5-V-002, both docs-honesty findings, both fixed in-session; R2 (narrow, fixes-only) VERIFIED with both ids RESOLVED and no new defect. Path-aware critique round lineage (L-070-1: final round is the committed artifact, lineage recorded here): R1 both arms ran but the producer's identity guard refused the write (--level required vs recorded policy 'none' - the spec prose said 'required' but the config block never seeded pathAwareCritique, so the immutable set-start record is 'none'; spec-authoring gap flagged for the Step 9 review); R2 wrote a valid artifact but the instruction framed only S4's close summary (the producer fills from disposition.json); R3 (final, committed) ran after a preliminary set-wide disposition summary was authored: gemini-2.5-pro VERIFIED 0 findings, gpt-5.4 1 Major adjudicated accept-finding with false-premise notes via record_adjudication (fixture fixed; blocking premises refuted by grep/source evidence). Routed cost in router-metrics.jsonl for session 5: $0.25 (documentation gemini-pro $0.03 + gpt-5-4-mini auto-verify $0.04; session-verification gpt-5-4 $0.15 + $0.05). The three pull-critique producer runs (2 arms each) meter outside router-metrics.jsonl and are not in that figure. UAT REMEDIATION (post-R2): the operator's walk failed walks 4-5 on a REAL pre-existing defect - the PyPI install's config-seed one-liner printed the bundled router-config.yaml through the child Python's text-mode stdout, which is cp1252 on Windows; the config carries U+2192, so the one-liner crashed and the fail-open branch silently skipped the seed, leaving fresh scaffolds with NO workspace config (reproduced against the real published 0.28.0 wheel; same cp1252 class as Set 078's decode fix, encode side). Fixed: raw-bytes one-liner (READ_BUNDLED_ROUTER_CONFIG_CODE, Layer-2-pinned) + the failed seed now NAMED in the install message. Verification R3 (gpt-5-4, narrow) confirmed the fix and raised S5-V-003 (Major): per-chunk toString('utf8') corrupts multibyte sequences split across chunk boundaries - fixed class-wide via the shared makeUtf8ChunkDecoder (StringDecoder) at all five spawner sinks, 4-test unit suite added. R4 (narrow) VERIFIED, S5-V-003 RESOLVED, no regression. Layer 2 1244 passing; tsc clean. Additional routed cost: session-verification gpt-5-4 R3 $0.08 + R4 $0.07.
blockers: Operator RE-WALK of UAT walks 4 and 5 against a REBUILT VSIX (the first walk found a real pre-existing defect - the cp1252 config-seed crash left fresh scaffolds with no ai_router/router-config.yaml; fixed in verification rounds 3-4, VERIFIED)
Operator clarification of UAT walk 3 (recorded not-passing with no feedback attached)
Operator decision on the requested table-style reformatting of the two second-level radio groups (follow-up set vs. hold this release)
Operator authorization for the vsix-v0.35.0 tag push (extension-only release; router stays 0.28.0)
